raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
October 2024 actuals came in at $577,500 against a budget of $680,000, a shortfall of $102,500 (-15.1%), driven almost entirely by a revenue miss of $82,000 that cascaded into lower Professional Services and Cost of Revenue. The revenue miss comprises $70,000 in identifiable deal slippage across three accounts (Glenbrook Financial, Axis Media Group, and Summit Retail Corp) and a permanent loss of $12,000 (Clearwater Tech, lost to a competitor on price). Downstream lines partially recovered the picture on cost, but the slipped deals carry residual close risk, particularly Summit Retail Corp, which requires monitoring into November.

LINE COMMENTARY

Revenue | -$82,000 (-16.4%) | Budget: $500,000 | Actual: $418,000
The $82,000 shortfall has two distinct components: $70,000 in slipped deals and $12,000 in permanent loss.
- Slipped deals ($70,000): Glenbrook Financial ($45,000, Nov 15 target, 95% probability — procurement signature pending), Axis Media Group ($28,000, Dec 1 target, 80% probability — new procurement lead engaged), and Summit Retail Corp ($9,000, Nov 30 target, 60% probability — champion changed, re-demo scheduled Nov 20). These are expected to close in November/December but carry varying levels of residual risk, and Summit in particular may not recover at full value.
- Permanent loss ($12,000): Clearwater Tech was lost to competitor Apex AI on price and should be treated as a permanent revenue reduction with no recovery expected.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-07 - crm-revenue-broad - crm-oct-pipeline: October pipeline close analysis. 3 deals pushed from October to November/December: Glenbrook Financial (ARR $45,000), Axis Media Group (ARR $28,000), Summit Retail Corp (ARR $9,000). One deal lost: Clearwater Tech ($12,000 ARR) to competitor Apex AI on price. Total miss: $82,000.
- CRM - 2024-11-07 - crm-revenue-narrow - crm-oct-deals-detail: Glenbrook — 95% probability, procurement sign-off pending. Axis — 80% probability, new procurement lead responsive. Summit — 60% probability after champion change, re-demo scheduled Nov 20. Total slipped pipeline in firm forecast: $73,000. Clearwater loss is permanent.
- Slack - 2024-11-07 - slack-revenue-broad - sales-slack-2024-10-31: October close recap — we missed plan by $82K. Three slips and one loss. Summit is a re-qualify risk. Clearwater went to Apex on price — nothing more we could do.

Professional Services | -$6,000 (-12.0%) | Budget: $50,000 | Actual: $44,000
The $6,000 shortfall is downstream of the revenue deal slippage: two SOW renewals — Glenbrook ($3,500) and Axis ($2,500) — are ready to execute but cannot be invoiced until the primary software deals close, and a third PS engagement is pending the Summit Retail Corp onboarding. Recovery is likely in November alongside the primary deal closings, though it is subject to the same close-risk caveats noted for Revenue.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-21 - crm-professional_services-broad - crm-ps-oct: Two SOW renewals (Glenbrook $3,500, Axis $2,500) held pending primary deal close — will be booked when revenue deals sign. One new PS engagement delayed due to onboarding dependency on Summit close.
- Slack - 2024-11-21 - slack-professional_services-broad - ps-slack-2024-10-31: PS miss is entirely downstream of software deals. Glenbrook and Axis PS SOWs are ready to execute but we can't invoice until the primary deals close.

Cost of Revenue | -$12,000 (-15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $68,000
The $12,000 underspend reflects variable COGS scaling with reduced new customer onboardings in October: the Glenbrook and Axis enterprise onboardings, and their associated hosting and support costs, were pushed into November alongside their deals. This is corroborated by CRM; no Slack context was found for this line, so the evidence is single-source (CRM only).
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-15 - crm-cost_of_revenue-broad - crm-cor-oct: $12,000 underspend reflects lower hosting and support costs from reduced new customer onboardings — two enterprise onboardings (Glenbrook, Axis) pushed to November with their deals. Variable COGS scales with revenue recognition.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
- Software Licenses: -$3,000 (-10.0%) — below the significance threshold; no detailed investigation performed.
- Support Contracts: +$500 (+2.5%) — below the significance threshold; no detailed investigation performed.
